title: Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards: government response to report
description: The government's response to the Committee's 2020 report Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards
summary: The government has responded to a report on Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards, acknowledging the importance of developing guidelines and regulations for the safe and responsible use of AI. The response aims to address concerns and recommendations made in the original report to promote trust and transparency in AI systems.

title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: This document assesses the impact of using copyrighted works in the development of artificial intelligence systems. Its purpose is to examine and report on how copyright law affects AI development and use.

title: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in education
description: The Department for Education’s (DfE) position on the use of generative AI in the education sector.
summary: The Department for Education has a policy on using generative artificial intelligence in education to guide its use and development. The policy likely covers the role of AI in teaching and learning.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: This document outlines the Department for Transport's plan to harness the benefits of artificial intelligence in the transport sector while minimizing potential dangers. It details how the DfT intends to collaborate with the transport industry to make the most of AI's capabilities.

title: Copyright and artificial intelligence: progress report
description: A progress report on the use of copyright works in the development of AI systems, under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.
summary: This report discusses the use of copyrighted works in developing artificial intelligence systems as required by law. It presumably provides updates and findings on how these laws are being implemented.

title: The regulation of artificial intelligence as a medical device: government response to the RHC
description: Government response to the recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council (RHC) on the regulation of AI as a medical device.
summary: The government has released a policy document outlining its response to a council's recommendations on regulating artificial intelligence (AI) used in medical settings. This policy aims to address concerns about the safety and effectiveness of AI in medical devices.

title: ​​Ofqual’s approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector​
description: Published in response to a request from the Secretary of State for Education and the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology.
summary: This document outlines Ofqual's approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence within the qualifications sector. It aims to provide guidance following demands from the Secretary of State for Education and the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology.

title: Government response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence
description: Government response to the Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence report ‘AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency’.
summary: The UK government has issued a response to the House of Lords Select Committee's report on Artificial Intelligence, focusing on ways to maintain a proactive stance in the sector. The response aims to address concerns and implement changes in line with the committee's recommendations to ensure AI is used responsibly and positively in the UK.

title: AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference
description: Terms of Reference and FAQs for the Committee's review into artificial intelligence and public standards.
summary: This document outlines the terms of reference for a committee reviewing artificial intelligence and its relationship with public standards. It likely provides a framework for the committee's investigation and a set of questions they will consider during their review.

title: CSPL submission to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on Governance of AI
description: CSPL has submitted written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on Governance of Artificial Intelligence.
summary: The CSPL has submitted evidence to an inquiry about how artificial intelligence should be governed in the UK. This submission likely contains the CSPL's views on AI regulation and oversight.
